title: Ctenium carolinianum
intro: Ctenium carolinianum, also known as Eastern Dropseed or Carolina Dropseed, is a perennial grass native to the southeastern United States. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to have potential health benefits in relieving symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ctenium carolinianum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture in traditional herbal remedies, as well as being used in the production of teas and cough syrups to help alleviate respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Ctenium carolinianum is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Ctenium, which includes several species of grasses found in various parts of the world. The genus Ctenium is further classified within the subfamily Chloridoideae. This classification reflects the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other grasses. The classification of Ctenium carolinianum is also subject to ongoing research and refinement in the field of botany.
uses: Ctenium carolinianum is a plant species known to have various traditional medicinal uses. In some parts of its native range, it is used to treat fever, colds, and other respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and stems are often made into teas or infusions to help alleviate symptoms. Ctenium carolinianum is also used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues, such as nausea and diarrhea. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential interactions.

preparations: Ctenium carolinianum, also known as Carolina feather grass, is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world.
